title: fagraea megalantha
intro: Fagraea megalantha, a flowering plant species native to the tropical forests of Malaysia, is a member of the Gentianaceae family and is also known as the "Malaysian frangipani". It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The plant's extracts have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting against bacterial and fungal infections, and protecting against oxidative stress. Fagraea megalantha is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
